mechanism_narrative: Persistently low unemployment signals tight labor markets, boosting consumer income and spending, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0% over any consecutive 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, it signals restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs, depresses investment, and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC below -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate synchronized economic expansion, which boosts corporate revenue and profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal (both INDPRO and RSAFS rising month-over-month) fails to produce positive median 20-day returns over any 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 4%), rising housing starts indicate overheating in the housing market that prompts the Fed to tighten, ultimately depressing equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when UNRATE < 4% and HOUST increases from prior month, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more often than negative (i.e., at least 55% of instances).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the CPI (rise > 0.4%) signals inflationary pressure that erodes real earnings and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward S&P 500 return after CPI monthly changes >0.4% is not negative or is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline while the federal funds rate rises, it indicates simultaneous weakening housing demand and monetary tightening, reinforcing negative economic expectations and reducing equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when HOUST declines month-over-month and FEDFUNDS increases, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive in more than 40% of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth indicates overall economic expansion, which directly lifts corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward S&P 500 return following positive quarterly GDP growth is negative more than 40% of the time over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is elevated (above 110 index), rising retail sales reflect robust consumer demand that validates existing production capacity and points to sustained economic growth, boosting equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In the regime where INDPRO > 110, the combined signal of rising RSAFS and stable or rising INDPRO yields negative median 20-day returns over any 3-year walk-forward period.
